review_summary: The transcript and unit summary provide enough grounded detail to generate 4 reinforcement questions about adding positional information and nonlinearity without requiring a separate video clip.

question_bank.question: Why can't plain self-attention alone distinguish "Zuko made his uncle" from "his uncle made Zuko"?
choices: Because plain self-attention is permutation-invariant unless order information is added
Because self-attention always sorts words alphabetically
Because the model cannot represent names and pronouns together
Because feed-forward networks remove all token identity
question_bank.explanation: Without positional information, self-attention treats the input as a set of token vectors. Reordering the same tokens can therefore produce the same underlying interactions.

question_bank.question: What simple fix does the unit introduce to encode order in transformers?
choices: Add a position vector p_i to each token embedding x_i
Sort tokens by frequency before attention
Use only the final token for prediction
question_bank.explanation: Adding a positional vector to each token lets the model represent both which word it is and where it appears in the sequence.

question_bank.question: What is one practical drawback of learned positional embeddings mentioned in the unit?
choices: They impose a maximum supported sequence length
They prevent attention from using values
question_bank.explanation: Learned positional embeddings allocate a learned vector for each allowed position up to some maximum n, so inputs longer than that limit are not directly supported.

question_bank.question: Why is a position-wise feed-forward network added after self-attention?
choices: Because repeated attention alone mostly keeps re-averaging values and needs extra nonlinearity
Because attention cannot handle more than one token at a time
Because positional encodings must be recomputed by an MLP
question_bank.explanation: Self-attention is powerful for mixing information across positions, but by itself it mainly forms weighted averages. A feed-forward MLP adds nonlinear computation at each position.
